channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Super Bowl LIX: Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce spotted out in New Orleans
item.description: Power couple Taylor Swift and Kansas City Chiefs Tight End Travis Kelce were spotted on Friday night eating at a restaurant while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX.
item.dc:creator: Kenny Lopez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Here's what we know about a commuter plane crash in Alaska that killed 10 people
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — Authorities are working to recover the wreckage of a plane crash in western Alaska that killed 10 people while investigators are trying to determine what caused the small commuter aircraft to go down in the icy Bering Sea.
item.dc:creator: BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing Alaska flight found with no survivors: What to know
item.description: The plane, operated by Bering Air, had been traveling from Unalakleet to Nome when it vanished on Thursday, only to be found the next day.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, KREX 5

item.title: Tips to easily cancel your subscriptions
item.description: The FTC says that it received about 70 consumer complaints a day in 2024 about recurring subscriptions, up from 42 per day in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Kimberly Palmer, NewsNation

item.title: 2 people charged in fatal stabbing of Fort Campbell soldier in Tennessee, police say
item.description: Police say two people have been charged in the death of a Fort Campbell soldier who was stabbed nearly 70 times.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ohio city files federal lawsuit against alleged neo-Nazi group
item.description: The city of Springfield, Ohio has filed a lawsuit against an alleged neo-Nazi group for conducting what it claims to be a campaign of threats and intimidation.
item.dc:creator: LeAnne Marie McPherson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's DEI order leaves academic researchers fearful of political influence over grants
item.description: BERKELEY, Calif. (AP) — President Donald Trump’s crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives in programs receiving federal money has thrown into doubt the future of research Kendra Dahmer has been doing on intestinal parasites in India and Benin.
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, TERRY CHEA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump says he is firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Survivor of 1982 DC plane crash credits pilot school lesson with saving his life
item.description: After a D.C. plane collision Jan. 29, Joseph Stiley, survivor of an eerily similar 1982 plane crash in D.C., remembers how he fought to survive in the freezing Potomac.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Child-abusing mommy bloggers Ruby Franke and friend sued over alleged fraud scheme targeting parents
item.description: A Utah man claims he lost more than $1 million after his spouse became enthralled with disgraced Utah parenting bloggers Ruby Franke and Jodi Hildebrandt's bizarre "counseling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge blocks Elon Musk's DOGE from accessing sensitive US Treasury Department material
item.description: A federal judge early Saturday blocked Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency from accessing Treasury Department records that contain sensitive personal data such as Social Security and bank account numbers for millions of Americans. U.S. District Judge Paul A.
item.dc:creator: ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job. U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: For high school sports, decisions loom: Follow Trump or state law on transgender athletes
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota's governing body for high school sports says it will follow state law — not President Donald Trump's executive order — and continue to allow transgender athletes to compete in prep athletics.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: States absorb big increases in Medicaid for sicker-than-expected enrollees after COVID-19 pandemic
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — States are absorbing substantial increases in health care costs for the poor, as they realize that the people remaining on Medicaid rolls after the COVID-19 pandemic are sicker than anticipated — and costlier to care for.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump is trying to halt the EV charger buildout. Experts say it's not that easy
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Experts are questioning President Donald Trump’s latest effort to slow the electric vehicle charging buildout in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Latino group calls for boycott of Coca-Cola, Walmart and more over deportations
item.description: This is part of an effort by a group called Freeze Latino Movement to boycott American companies while poking fun of the ongoing deportations launched by the Trump Administration and Immigration and Customs Enforcement, ICE.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alaska Legislature asks Trump to retain Denali's name instead of change it to Mount McKinley
item.description: JUNEAU, Alaska (AP) — The Alaska Legislature passed a resolution Friday urging President Donald Trump to reverse course and retain the name of North America’s tallest peak as Denali rather than change it to Mount McKinley.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say Huntsville real estate agent was abducted, warrants obtained for 5 in investigation
item.description: Police are now saying a Huntsville real estate agent reported missing nearly two months ago was abducted, marking a "significant shift" in the case from early reports it did not appear he was in any danger.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Record number of employees expected to call off work after Super Bowl LIX, survey says
item.description: Whether you are tuning in for the game, halftime show, or commercials, many people are looking forward to Super Bowl Sunday. However, those same people often loathe clocking into work the following morning.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Naval Academy closing DEI offices to align with Trump executive orders: memo
item.description: The head of the U.S. Naval Academy says the school is closing DEI offices and ending DEI-related contracts to comply with President Donald Trump's executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for review of all US funding to NGOs
item.description: President Trump on Friday called for a review of federal funding to nongovernmental organizations (NGOs) and for a halt on spending to groups that “undermine the national interest.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Louisiana woman arrested following death of Kansas City reporter covering Super Bowl LIX
item.description: A Louisiana woman has been arrested after she was allegedly caught using the reporter's credit cards in the New Orleans area.
item.dc:creator: Raeven Poole, KRON4

item.title: Sports reporter dies while in New Orleans for Super Bowl LIX
item.description: Adan Manzano died on Wednesday in New Orleans while on assignment to cover the Super Bowl for both Telemundo Kansas City and Tico Sports.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Mueller, ABC 4

item.title: 500,000 LG electric ovens recalled after fires, pet deaths
item.description: There are 86 reports of unintentional activation of the front knobs, 28 fires, at least eight injuries, and three pet deaths, per the Consumer Product Safety Commission.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, ABC 4

item.title: Baseball star Shohei Ohtani’s ex-interpreter is sentenced to nearly 5 years in sports betting case
item.description: SANTA ANA, Calif. (AP) — The former interpreter for baseball star Shohei Ohtani was sentenced Thursday to nearly five years in prison for bank and tax fraud after he stole nearly $17 million from the Los Angeles Dodgers player’s bank account.
item.dc:creator: AMY TAXIN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Connecticut's Bradley International Airport experiencing air traffic controller shortage
item.description: Numbers obtained by NewsNation affiliate WTNH show that one senator is quite concerned about airline safety.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Derderian, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Project 2025 architect Russell Vought to lead powerful White House budget office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Russell Vought as White House budget director on Thursday night, putting an official who has planned the zealous expansion of President Donald Trump's power into one of the most influential positions in the federal government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: San Jose State and Penn face inquiries in Trump crackdown on transgender athletes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is investigating potential civil rights violations at two universities and a high school sports league that allowed transgender athletes to compete on women's teams, the Education Department said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE was tasked with stopping Treasury payments to USAID, AP sources say
item.description: Officials working with Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency sought access to the U.S. Department of Treasury payment system to stop money from flowing to the U.S. Agency for International Development, according to two people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: WFLA, NBC

item.title: NCAA changes transgender policy to limit women's competition to athletes assigned female at birth
item.description: The NCAA changed its participation policy for transgender athletes on Thursday, limiting competition in women's sports to athletes assigned female at birth only.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Applebee’s brings back Date Night Passes
item.description: Applebee’s announced Thursday that it's bringing back the coveted Date Night Pass, which went viral on social media during its debut last year.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Superintendent forum interrupted by pornographic image
item.description: An online superintendent forum at noon Tuesday was interrupted by a pornographic image, according to a spokesperson with the Liberty County School System.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Smith, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Virginia Halas McCaskey, longtime Bears owner and matriarch, dies at 102, team announces
item.description: LAKE FOREST, Ill. — Virginia Halas McCaskey, the longtime owner and matriarch of the Chicago Bears, died Thursday at 102, the team announced in a news release. Virginia served as the owner of the Bears since her father and team founder George S. Halas died on Oct. 31, 1983.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Japan Airlines plane clips Delta flight at Sea-Tac airport
item.description: A plane at Seattle-Tacoma International Airport hit another aircraft that was taxiing on the runway Wednesday morning, the Federal Aviation Administration has confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Honolulu records 6th death from massive explosion of illegal fireworks over New Year's
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — A sixth person died Wednesday from injuries sustained when crates of illegal fireworks ignited during a New Year’s Eve party in a Honolulu neighborhood, setting off a chain of explosions that left more than a dozen people with severe burns.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police in Wisconsin give update on missing pregnant teenager, vehicle using multiple license plates
item.description: (WFRV) - The 2014 Black Buick LaCrosse involved in an AMBER Alert for a missing pregnant 16-year-old girl from Beaver Dam is reportedly using multiple license plates.
item.dc:creator: Adam Rosen, WGN 9

item.title: A timeline of last week's air disaster in Washington, the deadliest in the US since 2001
item.description: It has been nearly a week since a commercial jetliner and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington’s Ronald Reagan National Airport, killing 67 people in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4
